sglang.title: Fix perf regression on small batch sizes due to kv cache scale
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix a regression introduced by #2786
python3 -m sglang.bench_offline_throughput --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --dataset-name random --num-p 1 --random-input 32 --random-output 512 --random-range 1 --tp 8 --enable-torch-compile --cuda-graph-max-bs 1

before the fix: 438 token/s
after the f

sglang.title: Roll back to use vllm custom allreduce
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix perf regression by rolling back to use vllm custom allreduce

To reproduce the regression:python3 -m sglang.bench_offline_throughput --model meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct --dataset-name random --num-p 1 --random-input 32 --random-output 512 --random-range 1 --tp 8 --enable-torch-compile --cud

sglang.title: Removing device sync overhead
sglang.author: Edenzzzz
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Reopening #2533 as per discussion with @xiezhq-hermann.
Modifications
Device sync waits for all other functions to complete, potentially computing much longer runtime than a single function has.
In the future, we might also need to specify the stream of the func, since load and write-thro

sglang.author: ByronHsu

Modifications


Expose worker startup secs
Also improve error handling. To be specific, originally we panic for error in router init, but py is not able to catch panic error. Therefore, I switched to return stderr to make py side a

vllm.title: [misc] add placeholder format.sh
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
there are many places referring to format.sh , and people are also familiar with format.sh .
let's put a placeholder and give people clear guidance on how to use the new linting system.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Remove dummy CI steps
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: We have changed the required checks from ruff + yapf to pre-commit, so these dummy steps can be removed now.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Make pre-commit faster
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Running mypy on all target Python versions takes too long for local development.
This PR reserves manual stage to be only run in pre-commit CI, and moves the mypy checks to manual stage. Meanwhile, a new commit hook is added to run mypy only on the current Python version. This hook is assigned to pr

vllm.title: [Model] Upgrade Aria to transformers 4.48
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The Aria HF repo has removed their model and config files, so now it is required to use transformers 4.48 to load the model. This PR updates the model and config definitions accordingly and temporarily patches huggingface/transformers#35768 so that we can still run the model in vLLM.

vllm.title: [misc] print a message to suggest how to bypass commit hooks
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
developers find it annoying if they need to pass linters for temporary commits.
not everyone knows the --no-verify falg.
I added this dummy hook to always print a message to tell users how to bypass hooks.

vllm.title: [core][bugfix] configure env var during import vllm
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: @jeejeelee found that these env vars are not set early enough. since vllm/__init__.py will always be executed, we can place these code here.

vllm.title: [V1] Remove `_get_cache_block_size`
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt: After #11960, the _get_cache_block_size is replaced by get_kv_cache_config. Remove this function for simplification.

vllm.title: [Misc] Pass `attention` to impl backend
vllm.author: wangxiyuan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
With #11969, a quantization method can be implemented and register to vLLM out-of-tree now. But there is no way to use the registered parameter for attention layer in custom attention backend impl.
This PR pass the attention object to attention backend, so that the backend can use the parameters reg

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix `HfExampleModels.find_hf_info`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix a CI bug introduced in #12203
Multi-modal tests should pass for real now.

vllm.title: [CI] Pass local python version explicitly to pre-commit mypy.sh
vllm.author: heheda12345
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
pre-commit passes all modified fills to mypy.sh, and $1 $2 will be filled by the path to modified filenames and I get the following errors:
tools/mypy.sh: line 12: [: vllm/v1/attention/backends/flash_attn.py: integer expression expected
usage: mypy [-h] [-v] [-V] [more options; see below]

vllm.title: [Misc] Update CODEOWNERS
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR adds codeowners for multimodal section as well as updating the github handles for neural magic folks post acquisition 😄

    return fn(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/p1/miniconda3/lib/python3.12/site-packages/vllm/entrypo

vllm.title: [misc] add cuda runtime version to usage data
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: add this usage data to help us decide whether to drop the support of cuda 11.8 .

vllm.title: [bugfix] catch xgrammar unsupported array constraints
vllm.author: Jason-CKY
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Catch xgrammar unsupported array constraints to fallback to outlines
FIX #12201

vllm.title: [Kernel] optimize moe_align_block_size for cuda graph and large num_experts (e.g. DeepSeek-V3)
vllm.author: jinzhen-lin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The moe_align_block_size is used by many moe models (e.g. DeepSeek-V3) but it is not compliable with cuda graph now, and its efficiency is relatively low when num_experts is high.
This PR optimizes moe_align_block_size from three aspects:

Replace cudaMalloc with torch::empty, so that we can enable

vllm.title: Add quantization and guided decoding CODEOWNERS
vllm.author: mgoin

vllm.title: [AMD][Build] Porting dockerfiles from the ROCm/vllm fork
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
An attempt to unify the build process with how it is done in ROCm/vllm
Split the build process into:

Building the required dependency libraries using Dockerfile.rocm_base. Not needed for the end user, done by AMD.
Building the vLLM itself on top of it.

In addition to not building the libraries eac

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix GGUF tp>1 models when vocab_size is not divisible by 64
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #12170
In VocabParallelEmbedding, when loading GGUF models weights, I believe param should be of shape padded_size/tp_size, as per class docs. Currently param is being instantiated as *unpadded_size*/tp_size https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/model_executor/layers/vocab_parallel

vllm.title: [ci/build] disable failed and flaky tests
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: these two tests failed a lot recently. while I don't have bandwidth to investigate further, let's disable them for now, until they are fixed.

vllm.title: [Misc] Rename `MultiModalInputsV2 -> MultiModalInputs`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Two months have passed since #10040, so I think it's safe to replace the old name now.

vllm.title: [Misc]Add BNB quantization for PaliGemmaForConditionalGeneration
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #12216
Using paligemma2-3b-ft-docci-448, I can generate reasonable results locally when using TP=1/2/4
